title: monelasmum unilaterale
side_effects: Monelasmum unilaterale can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic alkaloids. Ingesting large amounts of the plant's extract can lead to severe diarrhea, stomach cramps, and vomiting, as well as potentially causing long-term damage to the digestive system. Improper use of the plant has also been linked to allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Furthermore, Monelasmum unilaterale can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, which can exacerbate underlying health conditions.
